suggestion.analysis: Current list dies because 4 Block is not enough to cover repeated 16-damage turns, while 6 Strike still cannot race 72 HP fast enough. Logs show many turns with only 5 block into 16, causing 11 damage, and Scouts often spend energy without fixing survivability. Upgrade damage density with Smash and improve defense with Wall, cutting Scouts and some Strikes.

Losses are mostly close, with boss left at 3-8 HP, but the deck still takes too many unblocked 8s/16s when defense and offense clump awkwardly. In several losses, extra Wall copies caused low-tempo turns or stranded Smashes, while only 2 cheap Blocks made flexible defense inconsistent. Shift 1 Wall into 1 Block to smooth turns: Block pairs better with Smash, double-Block can cover 8s, and Block+Wall fully covers 16 while preserving hand efficiency. Keep the strong 4 Smash core since damage is already close to lethal.

Iteration 1 was much stronger than the current deck, so revert the failed Block-for-Wall swap. The loss logs show the core issue is dying on turn-6 16-damage attacks after drawing only Block or no defense; several losses would have been materially better with a second Wall available. Current 3 Block / 1 Wall overexposes the deck to 16s while not improving damage enough to compensate.

Current 4 Smash / 4 Strike / 2 Block / 2 Wall is close, but losses repeatedly come from drawing pure offense on turn 3 or 6 and taking full 16. History says cutting a Wall was disastrous, so keep 2 Wall. The logs also show several awkward hands with double Smash and unused energy, plus some losses at 3-8 boss HP, suggesting a small consistency upgrade is better than more raw damage. Add Scout to dig for Wall/Block on 16 turns and smooth stranded energy, trimming one Strike and one Smash rather than defense.

The Scout package clearly hurt both damage density and survival. Losses frequently spent turn 4/5/7 on Scout or Wall+Scout with little pressure, then died with boss at 8-13 HP. History also shows the best prior deck was the pure 4 Smash / 4 Strike / 2 Block / 2 Wall build, and both deviations from it performed much worse. Reverting to that stronger baseline is the most evidence-based improvement.

Deck is already very strong at 90% win rate, so only a small, evidence-based tweak is warranted. The lone loss died on turn 6 to a 16 because no defense was drawn; however, history strongly shows that replacing attacks with Scout or shifting to 3 Block/1 Wall was much worse. The best-supported adjustment is a minimal swap from one Strike to one Block: it slightly improves odds of having some defense on turns 3/6 while preserving the proven 4 Smash and 2 Wall core. This may trade a little speed for a better chance to avoid the specific lethal pattern seen in the only loss.

Iteration history shows the pure 4 Smash / 4 Strike / 2 Block / 2 Wall build was the best deck by far (90% win rate), and the latest tweak to +1 Block -1 Strike collapsed to 30%. The current loss logs support reverting: many losses leave the boss at 2-14 HP, meaning the deck now lacks finishing damage more than it lacks defense. Extra Block often produced low-pressure turns, while even with 3 Block and 2 Wall the deck still frequently failed to fully cover 16s. The strongest evidence-based move is to restore the proven high-damage baseline rather than experiment further.

Current build underperformed its earlier 90% result, but the loss logs still show the same core pattern: deaths usually happen because defense is absent on turn 3/6, while boss HP is often left in the 3-14 range. History strongly says adding more Block or Scout hurt win rate, and cutting Wall was disastrous. The best-supported move is a very small speed increase: convert 1 Block into 1 Strike. This keeps the important 2 Wall for 16-damage coverage, preserves 4 Smash, and improves odds of finishing before the boss gets extra attacks.

Current 1 Block / 2 Wall defense is too thin and the logs show repeated deaths on turn 6 with boss at 3 HP; many losses are exactly one partial block or one extra defensive draw away from survival. History says the best-performing family keeps 4 Smash and 2 Wall, while adding too much Block or any Scout hurts badly. So I’d undo the last failed greed change and restore one Block by cutting one Strike.

Most losses are still from missing enough defense on turn 3/6, but history is very clear that adding Scout or shifting further toward Block hurts win rate badly. The current list also loses several games with boss at 3-14 HP, so cutting damage is risky. The best evidence-backed move is a very small defense increase without touching the crucial 4 Smash core: swap 1 Strike for 1 Block to improve odds of partial coverage on 16 turns and survive close losses.
